name: Markdown to WeChat MCP Server
what_it_does: Markdown to WeChat MCP server enabling AI agents to publish content to WeChat Official Accounts (微信公众号) — converting Markdown content to WeChat's rich text format, uploading images, drafting articles in WeChat backend, and integrating WeChat Official Account content publishing into agent-driven content creation workflows.
use_cases: Publishing AI-generated articles to WeChat Official Account from content agents
Converting Markdown blog posts to WeChat format from content publishing agents
Scheduling and drafting WeChat articles from editorial workflow agents
Automating WeChat content distribution from marketing agents
Cross-posting content from other platforms to WeChat from syndication agents
Building content pipelines for Chinese audience from media production agents
not_for: Non-WeChat content platforms (use Notion, WordPress, or Ghost MCPs)
Teams without WeChat Official Account registration
Personal WeChat (not Official Account/公众号) publishing
best_when: A Chinese content creator or brand with a WeChat Official Account needs AI agents to draft and publish content in WeChat's format from Markdown source.
avoid_when: You don't have a WeChat Official Account, or target audiences outside China — use platform-agnostic content MCPs.

auth.notes: WeChat Official Account App ID and App Secret required. Access token obtained via WeChat API. Token expires every 2 hours — automatic refresh needed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: WeChat API: free with Official Account registration
pricing.paid_tiers: WeChat Official Account: free subscription/service accounts
pricing.notes: WeChat Official Account registration requires China business or personal verification. Community MCP is free.

performance.rate_limits: WeChat API: 500 draft creations/day, 48 publishes/month for subscription accounts

known_agent_gotchas: WeChat access token expires every 2 hours — implement automatic refresh
Monthly publish limits apply — subscription accounts limited to 48/month
WeChat API documentation primarily in Chinese
Image upload required before referencing in articles — images can't be URLs
Community MCP — WeChat API changes can break publishing workflows
WeChat content review policies may reject certain content types
agent_readiness.security_notes: WeChat App ID and Secret — store securely. HTTPS for WeChat API. Content published to Chinese platform subject to WeChat content policies. Community MCP. Token refresh logic critical for security.

security_score_components.security_notes: WeChat App ID/Secret. HTTPS. Content on WeChat servers. Token expiry handling. Community MCP.
